---
title: "Impact of Social Media Usage on Academic Performance of College Students"
output:
  word_document: default
  pdf_document: default
date: "2026-05-30"
---
# Project Overview

This project analyzes the relationship between social media usage and academic performance among college students.

A dataset of 50 students was created containing:

- Social Media Hours
- Study Hours
- Sleep Hours
- Attendance
- Marks

The main objective is to determine whether increased social media usage affects academic performance.

# Descriptive Statistics

- Total Students: 50
- Mean Social Media Usage: 4.28 hours/day
- Median Social Media Usage: 4 hours/day
- Maximum Usage: 8 hours/day
- Minimum Usage: 1 hour/day

# Visual Analysis

The histogram shows the distribution of social media usage among students.

The boxplot summarizes the spread of social media usage and indicates there are no significant outliers.

# Correlation Analysis

The calculated correlation between Social Media Hours and Marks is:

**-0.99**

This indicates a very strong negative relationship.

# Key Observation

Students who spend more time on social media tend to score lower marks.

Students using 1–2 hours of social media generally scored above 85%.

Students using 7–8 hours generally scored below 65%.

# Conclusion

The analysis suggests that excessive social media usage may negatively impact academic performance.

A strong inverse relationship was observed between social media usage and marks.
